questions.question: What is Docker primarily used for?
What does a container virtualize?
What does a virtual machine virtualize?
How large are containers typically compared to virtual machines?
options: Containers are measured in gigabytes; VMs in megabytes
Containers are measured in megabytes; VMs in gigabytes
questions.answer: Containers are measured in megabytes; VMs in gigabytes

questions.question: How long does a container typically take to start compared to a VM?
options: Containers take minutes; VMs take seconds
Containers take seconds; VMs take minutes
questions.answer: Containers take seconds; VMs take minutes

questions.question: What level of isolation do containers provide?
What level of isolation do virtual machines provide?
What do containers share with the host system?
What is a Docker image?
options: A running instance of a container
A read-only template containing the OS, app, and dependencies
A configuration file for the Docker daemon
questions.answer: A read-only template containing the OS, app, and dependencies

questions.question: What is a Docker container?
options: A read-only template for creating images
A background service that manages images
A running instance of an image
A command-line tool for interacting with Docker
questions.answer: A running instance of an image

questions.question: What is the Docker daemon?
options: A background service that manages containers
questions.answer: A background service that manages containers

questions.question: What is the name of the Docker daemon process?
Using an OOP analogy, a Docker image is like a _____ and a container is like a _____.
What happens when you run 'docker run hello-world' and the image is not found locally?
options: Docker throws an error and exits
Docker builds the image from source
Docker downloads the image from Docker Hub
questions.answer: Docker downloads the image from Docker Hub

questions.question: What command shows all Docker images on the system?
What command shows only currently running containers?
What flag must you add to 'docker ps' to show stopped containers as well?
What does 'docker rm' do?
What does 'docker rmi' do?
What do the '-it' flags do when running 'docker run -it ubuntu bash'?
options: -i installs packages and -t tests the container
-i enables interactive mode (keeps STDIN open) and -t allocates a terminal (TTY)
-i initializes the image and -t tags the container
-i inspects the container and -t terminates it after running
questions.answer: -i enables interactive mode (keeps STDIN open) and -t allocates a terminal (TTY)

questions.question: If you install curl inside an Ubuntu container and then start a new Ubuntu container, what happens to curl?
options: Curl is available in the new container because it was saved to the image
Curl is not available because containers are ephemeral and changes do not persist
Curl is available only if Docker Hub cached the update
Curl must be uninstalled manually before starting a new container
questions.answer: Curl is not available because containers are ephemeral and changes do not persist

questions.question: What does the '-d' flag do in 'docker run -d nginx'?
options: Deletes the container after it stops
Downloads the image without running it
Runs the container in detached mode (background)
Enables debug mode for the container
questions.answer: Runs the container in detached mode (background)

questions.question: In the command 'docker run -p 8080:80 nginx', what does '-p 8080:80' mean?
options: Expose port 80 on the host and 8080 in the container
Map port 8080 on the host to port 80 in the container
Open ports 8080 through 80 on the container
Set the container process ID to 8080
questions.answer: Map port 8080 on the host to port 80 in the container

questions.question: What does the '--name' flag do in 'docker run --name my-nginx nginx'?
options: Assigns a hostname to the container's network interface
Gives the container a custom name
Tags the image with a new name
questions.answer: Gives the container a custom name

questions.question: What command stops a running container named 'my-nginx'?
